money_model: Invoicing is ORDER-DRIVEN and PRODUCT-PRICED. An invoice is raised from an order; the order's line total = product price x qty. So the billed amount is controlled by the product's price (or a per-line unit_price override), NOT by a single 'total' field. This differs fundamentally from TruckingOffice's single 'No Load Invoice' form.
spa_input_learning: transporters.io is a JS SPA. Setting input.value directly (which works on TruckingOffice's server-rendered Rails forms) does NOT reliably register here: the framework recomputes line totals from its own model, selecting a product auto-fills unit_price from the product's stored price (overriding a programmatic unit_price), and qty/row-total are JS-computed. RELIABLE INPUT REQUIRES real keyboard/mouse event simulation via the CDP Input domain (Input.dispatchMouseEvent to focus/click, Input.insertText / Input.dispatchKeyEvent to type) OR the native value-setter PLUS triggering the framework's exact change handlers. This is the next capability the Operator Brain needs to drive SPA TMSs; it is also a self-heal-worthy learning.

flow_order_to_invoice.note: one-time setup; empty on a fresh account
one-time setup; the product price is what flows to the order line

line_item.reveal_button: Add Product (type=button, reveals inline line sub-form)

flow_order_to_invoice.trigger: on the order page, the 'Create invoice' action: <a class='HANDLER_NEW_INVOICE' data-route='https://neyma.transporters.io/order/{order_id}/invoice/create' data-modal-title='New Invoice'>

fields.total_price/total_amount: auto-computed from the order (0 if the order line saved at 0)

flow_order_to_invoice.note: confirm an invoice exists for the order with total == the approved amount

nav_map.uninvoiced: /finance/uninvoiced (note: did NOT list a freshly-created order; invoicing is via the order's Create-invoice action)
